zh.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare是Hubert de Saint Clair的女兒，於大約1130年出生在英國。她於大約1161年與William de Langvalei I在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國艾可萊可登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare是Hubert de Saint Clair的女儿，于大约1130年出生在英国。她于大约1161年与William de Langvalei I在英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在英国艾可萊可登。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare was born about 1130, in England as the daughter of Hubert de Saint Clair. She married William de Langvalei I about 1161, in Great Bromley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Lexden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare est née environ 1130, à Angleterre, fille de Hubert de Saint Clair. Elle a épousé William de Langvalei I environ 1161, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Lexden, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare wurde um 1130 in England, als Tochter von Hubert de Saint Clair geboren. Sie hat um 1161 in Great Bromley, Essex, England William de Langvalei I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Lexden, Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare è nata il circa 1130 nel England come figlia di Hubert de Saint Clair. Lei sposato William de Langvalei I circa 1161, nel Great Bromley, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Lexden, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clareは約1130年にイギリスでHubert de Saint Clairの娘として生まれた。約1161年にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーでWilliam de Langvalei Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスエセックスレクスデンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare(은)는 대략 1130년 영국에서 Hubert de Saint Clair의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1161년 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 William de Langvalei I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 에세스 렉스든에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare nasceu aproximadamente 1130, em Inglaterra filha de Hubert de Saint Clair. Ela casou-se com William de Langvalei I aproximadamente 1161, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em Lexden, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare родилась около 1130 г. в местности Англия. Её отца звали Hubert de Saint Clair. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Langvalei I около 1161 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в местности лексден, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Gunnora St Clare nació aproximadamente 1130, en Inglaterra como hija de Hubert de Saint Clair. Se casó con William de Langvalei I aproximadamente 1161, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Lexden, Essex, Inglaterra.
